title: Window Functions, Analytical Grain, and Time-Aware SQL · Spiral Data Review
summary: Service leaders need rankings, running effort, previous-event comparisons, and rolling metrics without collapsing detail rows. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a time-aware service-level analytics report after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define window partition, order, and frame independently from final result ordering.
Choose ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, and NTILE with explicit tie semantics.
Use LAG, LEAD, FIRST_VALUE, and LAST_VALUE with deliberate ordering and frame behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.
